Investigated: outputs.tf only exposes Cloudflare zone_id + DNS record IDs, and no consumer for these exists anywhere in the repo -- the deploy chain (remote-apply.sh, chezmoi apply) has no DNS-time dependency on them. Traced to source: the original migrate-if-to-terraform proposal's Req-7 itself was speculative ('outputs.tf emits any values the deploy hook needs' -- no concrete value/need ever named), which is why it sat [deferred] since 2026-04-12. Closing as won't-do rather than wire speculative plumbing with no real consumer; infra/.tf-outputs.env still gets written by tf.sh on every apply if a real need surfaces later.

Implemented as scripts/view.sh v2 dispatch extension (single cohesive change, all 3 tasks share the same file per design.md's locked v2 decisions). Verified live: bat native multi-file concat + sequential glow chaining confirmed in a real tmux split (bat pane closed on q -> glow auto-rendered next file in same pane); chafa -f kitty half-block image render confirmed live (real 1x1 PNG rendered, held pane open via bash -c wrapper, closed on keypress); PDF/binary routed to mac-open (verified with real .zip under $HOME, opened via actual mac-open handoff); directory -> eza --tree | less -R confirmed live and paged. Also added set -g allow-passthrough on to tmux.conf.tmpl (if-dc4's tmux-side requirement) and a curated binary-extension fast path (bin/exe/zip/etc.) since testing surfaced a real gap where unknown-but-present extensions bypassed the mime-sniff safety net. Found + fixed a zsh-vs-bash read-flag incompatibility during live tmux testing (pane runs login shell zsh, not bash; read -rsn1 needed an explicit bash -c wrap).
